title: A Lawsuit to Save a Weather and Climate Research Center
summary: A group that runs a big science center called the National Center for Atmospheric Research (NCAR) is suing the Trump administration. The government wants to shut down NCAR, which studies weather, climate, and space weather. NCAR is in Boulder, Colorado, and has supercomputers and airplanes for research. Many scientists use it to learn about the atmosphere. The lawsuit says the government is closing NCAR without a good reason, just because President Trump is mad at Colorado. It also says the government broke rules by not explaining why and by telling people not to talk about it. The group hopes the lawsuit will stop the shutdown and save this important research center.

a science center that studies weather and climate

very powerful computers used for big science projects

questions.question: What rule does the lawsuit say the government broke by not explaining the shutdown?

background_read: The National Center for Atmospheric Research (NCAR) is a science center in Colorado that helps scientists study weather, climate, and space weather. It uses big computers and airplanes to do research. The government provides money to support NCAR, but the Trump administration wants to close it. A group called UCAR runs NCAR and is suing the government because they think the shutdown is unfair and breaks rules. This lawsuit is important because NCAR helps many scientists learn about our atmosphere and climate change.
Article_Structure: WHO: The University Corporation for Atmospheric Research (UCAR) and the Trump administration
WHAT: A lawsuit to stop the shutdown of the National Center for Atmospheric Research (NCAR)
WHERE: Boulder, Colorado, and in U.S. courts
WHY: Because the government is closing NCAR without a good reason and breaking rules

perspectives.description: Scientists and UCAR believe NCAR is important for research and should not be shut down unfairly.
The Trump administration thinks NCAR is not needed and is closing it, possibly for political reasons.
